Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This is a past-tense factual report of what was traced and fixed, not a suggestion or recommended action.
groundedness_reason: The ticket shows the Finance team was moved to a new AD group, the new group was a member of a Zurich location group causing the Zurich printer to be mapped via GPO and set as default, and removing/correcting the group membership (gpupdate and cleanup) restored the correct default printer.

Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21

Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52

Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.

12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: This simply reports a past cause and that applying fixes restored function, not offering or instructing future actions or recommendations.
groundedness_reason: SOURCE 22 documents printers moved to a separate VLAN, ping from the print server failing due to segmentation, confirmation that a firewall rule blocked server access, and that the rule was adjusted according to the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard which restored printing.

18.12.24 00:34 Re: TRAIN-CE/FO integration / MFA
Dear Employee 7,
During the joint analysis of the issue, we identified that two parameters were not correctly set during the ADFS client registration. In the new client application used specifically for the MFA request of the Toolbox on the Train system, the following parameters were set to true:
EnableJWT
RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders

EnableJWT activates the issuance of JSON Web Tokens in the authentication process. If enabled, ADFS issues JWTs instead of other token formats such as SAML. These tokens are evaluated by the target application. If the target system does not support JWTs or cannot validate their signature, authentication fails.

RequestMFAFromClaimsProviders forwards MFA requirements to configured claims providers. This delegates MFA execution to external providers and increases complexity in authentication flow, logging, and auditing.

The combination of these parameters can lead to login failures if the target system is not designed to handle JWTs or external MFA claims.

We have documented the configuration to avoid future issues. From our perspective, no further problems are expected after adjusting the parameters. If MFA works stably and the Train environment is reachable again, the login topic can be considered resolved.

Kind regards
Employee 21

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Location printer Basel not responding to print jobs
Ticket number: TI-1622483
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 15
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 06.11.25 08:37
Closure
Closure date: 12.11.25 14:52
Closure text: Network and queue configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Function restored.
Activities
06.11.25 08:25 – Email from Contact Person 15
Good morning
Since yesterday, the printer “MFP-Basel-01” is no longer responding to print jobs. The documents remain in the queue, and in some cases disappear without being printed.
Other printers in the building are functioning normally.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
06.11.25 08:37
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 21.
06.11.25 09:10 – Initial analysis
Remote access to an affected user.
Print job briefly appears in the print server queue, then disappears without printing.
Queue itself is not paused.
Print Spooler service is running.
Test print directly from the print server to the printer also fails.
06.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent changes to the network or infrastructure at the Basel location?
Thank you for your brief feedback.
06.11.25 11:20 – Customer response
Last week, a new network segment for devices was introduced. The printers were moved into a separate VLAN.
No other changes are known to us.
07.11.25 09:00 – Further analysis
Ping from print server to printer unsuccessful.
Ping from a client within the same location network successful.
This indicates segmentation between the server network and the printer VLAN.
Comparison with document “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Location Concept, it is defined that printers operate in a separate VLAN but must be reachable from the print server. Routing and firewall rules must be configured accordingly.
07.11.25 11:30 – Internal coordination with Network Team
Confirmation that the new firewall rule did not allow the server network access to the printer VLAN.
Rule adjusted according to standard.
10.11.25 – Test phase
Printing from print server successful.
Multiple test prints from user workstations performed.
No abnormalities observed.
12.11.25 14:40 – Customer feedback
Printer is functioning normally again.
Larger print jobs are also processed correctly.
12.11.25 14:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 15
The root cause was a missing network allowance between the print server and the newly configured printer VLAN at the Basel location.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”. The printer is fully reachable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

However, the claim that the current ticket reports multiple subsystems failing specifically including collaboration permissions and telephony/dialer is not present in the provided context.

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Incorrect printer automatically set as default
Ticket number: TI-1623017
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 18
Owner: Employee 23
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 18.11.25 08:49
Closure
Closure date: 24.11.25 15:11
Closure text: Printer assignment and default configuration corrected according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Behavior stable.
Activities
18.11.25 08:35 – Email from Contact Person 18
Good morning
Several employees in the Finance department report that their default printer automatically switches to “MFP-Zuerich-01”, although they work at the Basel location.
They must manually select the correct printer before each print job.
The issue seems to have started last week.
Kind regards
18.11.25 08:49
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 23.
18.11.25 09:20 – Initial analysis
Remote connection established with an affected user.
Default printer is indeed “MFP-Zuerich-01”.
Both Basel and Zurich printers are visible in the printer list.
Comparison with a user from another department shows the correct location printer set.
18.11.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Good day
Were there any recent organizational changes or group adjustments in the Finance department?
Kind regards
18.11.25 10:40 – Customer response
Yes, the Finance team was moved into a new AD group last week.
The old group was deactivated.
19.11.25 09:10 – GPO assignment analysis
Comparison with “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”.
In the section Printer Provisioning it is defined that:
• Printer assignments are location-based via AD groups
• Default printers per location are set via GPO
• No parallel groups with identical function may exist
Review shows that the new Finance group is additionally a member of a higher-level Zurich location group.
As a result, the Zurich printer is also mapped via GPO and set as default.
19.11.25 11:30 – Test
Temporary removal of the incorrect group membership.
gpupdate /force executed.
User logged in again.
Default printer now correctly remains “MFP-Basel-01”.
20.11.25 – Coordination with customer
Confirmation that the group structure had not been fully cleaned up during the reorganization.
Correction performed according to blueprint specification.
24.11.25 14:50 – Customer feedback
Multiple users tested.
Default printer remains correct.
No further automatic switching.
24.11.25 15:11 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 18
The root cause was an inconsistent group membership following an organizational adjustment.
Printer assignment was corrected according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
The correct location printer now remains permanently set as default.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
is_rec_reason: Describes required corrective actions (fix AD group membership and update GPO via gpupdate/test/login) as the resolution, which constitutes a recommendation for action.
groundedness_reason: Ticket TI-1623017 describes Basel users getting the Zurich default printer after the Finance team was moved into a new AD group that was also member of a Zurich location group; the fix removed the incorrect group membership, ran gpupdate /force and re-login, and the closure states resolution was performed according to the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Escalate to the M365 / Identity engineering team (in past incidents the BU Infrastructure – M365 / Identity group and system engineers handled comparable cases)

answer: TI-1700002: updated AAD Connect OU filter to include the new OU containing migration group objects and performed a full sync so group memberships propagated correctly to the cloud

SOURCE 54:
 06.01.25.
Thanks!

28.01.25 14:27 Ticket closed | TI-1700002
Closure description:
Root cause was that the new group structure created for the second migration wave was placed in an OU outside the Azure AD Connect organizational unit filter scope. The old groups remained synced because they were in the original sync scope. As a result, the cloud directory continued to evaluate users' membership against the (synchronized) old groups, while on-prem evaluated against the (renamed/recreated) new groups.
Resolution: Updated the Azure AD Connect OU filter to include the new "Migration_Phase2" OU containing the new group objects. Performed a full synchronization. Validated that affected users now have correct group memberships in both M365 and on-prem systems.

21.01.25 10:45 Employee 30
Reviewed AAD Connect configuration. The group filter (organizational unit-based) was set up for the original directory layout. The new groups for the second migration wave were placed in a new OU "Migration_Phase2" which was not included in the AAD Connect sync scope.

Updated OU filter to include Migration_Phase2. Ran initial delta sync to confirm new group objects appear in Entra ID, then ran full sync to ensure all member relationships propagate correctly.
27.01.25 09:30 Employee 30

Ticket closed.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: TI-1700004: created a KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA exclusion group, added service accounts, updated the Conditional Access policy to exclude that group, and reviewed AAD Connect sync scope; validated scheduled tasks resumed
context:
=== CITED SOURCES ===
SOURCE 50:
 you mention Conditional Access: as part of the migration baseline a CA policy was enabled requiring MFA for the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group. Could that be the issue?

31.01.25 16:34 Ticket closed | TI-1700004
Closure description:
Root cause was twofold. First, the service accounts were placed in the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group, which is the group targeted by the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy "KF70-Require-MFA". The Conditional Access evaluation was therefore being applied to non-interactive logons for service accounts, breaking scheduled-task authentication where MFA cannot be satisfied. Second, the service accounts had been moved to the new OU which is filtered IN by Azure AD Connect, leading to the accounts being synchronized to Entra ID and then having a license assigned (small but non-zero impact on the on-prem authentication path because of mfaPreferred attribute conflicts).
Resolution: Created a sub-group "KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA" containing the service accounts and added it to the Conditional Access policy as an explicit exclusion. Re-evaluated AAD Connect filter to keep the on-prem service-account authentication path clean. Validated that scheduled tasks are now running successfully overnight.
Documentation note: For future migrations, service accounts must be explicitly excluded from baseline CA policies via a dedicated exclusion group, and their AAD Connect sync status should be reviewed to avoid unintended cloud-side license assignment. We have added this to the migration runbook.

Notes:
21.01.25 08:02 Admin, CRM
Ticket opened.
21.01.25 08:20 Admin, CRM
Priority elevated due to operational impact.
24.01.25 10:00 Employee 30
Initial diagnosis: scheduled tasks fail with logon-type denied. Customer confirms recent migration changed service-account group membership. Strong suspicion the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy is being evaluated against non-interactive service-account logons.
27.01.25 14:30 Employee 30
Confirmed via Azure AD sign-in logs: failed sign-in attempts for the service accounts are tagged "Failed - Conditional Access requires MFA". The CA policy targets the All-Users-Hybrid group which includes the service accounts.
28.01.25 11:00 Employee 30
Created KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA exclusion group. Added service accounts. Updated CA policy to exclude this group. Sign-in logs now show successful authentication.
30.01.25 09:30 Employee 30
Customer confirms scheduled tasks ran successfully overnight without errors. Asked them to monitor for one more cycle before closure.
31.01.25 16:34 Microsoft CRM Portals
Ticket closed. Migration runbook updated with service-account handling guidance.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Scheduled tasks run by service accounts failing due to authentication aligns with a case where service accounts were unintentionally targeted by a tenant Conditional Access policy requiring MFA; resolution involved creating an exclusion group and adjusting CA/AAD Connect handling (TI-1700004). SOURCE 50:
 you mention Conditional Access: as part of the migration baseline a CA policy was enabled requiring MFA for the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group. Could that be the issue?

31.01.25 16:34 Ticket closed | TI-1700004
Closure description:
Root cause was twofold. First, the service accounts were placed in the "KF70-AllUsers-Hybrid" group, which is the group targeted by the new tenant-baseline Conditional Access policy "KF70-Require-MFA". The Conditional Access evaluation was therefore being applied to non-interactive logons for service accounts, breaking scheduled-task authentication where MFA cannot be satisfied. Second, the service accounts had been moved to the new OU which is filtered IN by Azure AD Connect, leading to the accounts being synchronized to Entra ID and then having a license assigned (small but non-zero impact on the on-prem authentication path because of mfaPreferred attribute conflicts).
Resolution: Created a sub-group "KF70-ServiceAccounts-NoMFA" containing the service accounts and added it to the Conditional Access policy as an explicit exclusion. Re-evaluated AAD Connect filter to keep the on-prem service-account authentication path clean. Validated that scheduled tasks are now running successfully overnight.
Documentation note: For future migrations, service accounts must be explicitly excluded from baseline CA policies via a dedicated exclusion group, and their AAD Connect sync status should be reviewed to avoid unintended cloud-side license assignment. We have added this to the migration runbook.

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: The inconsistent group memberships (on‑prem shows new groups while cloud still shows old groups) matches a case where the new group objects were placed in an OU outside the AAD Connect sync scope; the fix was to update the AAD Connect OU filter and perform a full sync (TI-1700002) — an infrastructure change handled by engineers

SOURCE 54:
 06.01.25.

28.01.25 14:27 Ticket closed | TI-1700002
Closure description:
Root cause was that the new group structure created for the second migration wave was placed in an OU outside the Azure AD Connect organizational unit filter scope. The old groups remained synced because they were in the original sync scope. As a result, the cloud directory continued to evaluate users' membership against the (synchronized) old groups, while on-prem evaluated against the (renamed/recreated) new groups.
Resolution: Updated the Azure AD Connect OU filter to include the new "Migration_Phase2" OU containing the new group objects. Performed a full synchronization. Validated that affected users now have correct group memberships in both M365 and on-prem systems.
